title: <div>  •  Exploratory: Can too much description overwhelm a reader? Where should
content:
• Exploratory: Can too much description overwhelm a reader? Where should writers draw the line between “showing” and “telling”?

Day 7: Dialogue and Voice in Narrative
• Concrete: Recall a memorable conversation you’ve had. Write a dialogue-heavy scene that captures the tone, rhythm, and personality of the exchange.
• Abstract: How does written dialogue differ from real-life speech? Should dialogue in stories mimic natural speech, or is it better to refine it?
• Connecting: Write a piece of dialogue between two characters where one is hiding something—how do you show deception through word choice and pacing?
• Exploratory: If you could have a conversation with your past or future self, what would you say?

Day 8: Revisiting and Refining Your Narrative
• Concrete: Look at the narrative topic you’ve chosen for this week’s rough draft. Write a new opening line for your essay—one that immediately grabs attention.
• Abstract: How do the first and last sentences of a story shape the reader’s experience? What makes a strong beginning and ending?
context: From document: Week 2 | Writing Warm-Ups — chunk 2 (chunk 2/3)
